identity.canonical_title: Gauss Linking Number and Topological Invariant

theory.canonical_statement: The Gauss linking number records the topological linking of two closed curves through the double contour integral $Lk=\frac{1}{4\pi}\oint_{C_1}\oint_{C_2}\frac{(\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2)\cdot(\mathrm{d}\mathbf{r}_1\times\mathrm{d}\mathbf{r}_2)}{|\mathbf{r}_1-\mathbf{r}_2|^3}$.
theory.technical_interpretation: For two closed field-line or flux-tube paths, the integral counts their oriented linking and is stable under continuous deformations that do not cut or intersect the curves.

existing_from_tex.description: Gauss linking number of the two closed curves.

symbol_definitions.Lk: Gauss linking number of the two closed curves

approved_source_enrichment.limitation: The aligned passage supports the Gauss linking integral and its variable roles; any application to a broader physical model remains separately reviewable.

dictionary_sections.definition: The Gauss linking number is an integer-valued invariant measuring oriented winding between two disjoint closed curves. It records linking that cannot be removed through continuous deformation without curve intersection.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: For closed field-line or flux-tube paths, \(Lk\) counts mutual linking. The curves may bend or move while retaining this number so long as they do not cross; appearance can change while topological connectedness remains fixed.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: Smooth deformations without intersection preserve \(Lk\). Reversing one curve's orientation reverses its sign; topologically unlinked curves have zero linking number under the standard reading.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The approved aligned source supplies the integral and identifies \(Lk\), paths \(C_1\), \(C_2\), and position vectors \(\mathbf{r}_1\), \(\mathbf{r}_2\). Later helicity readings remain applications.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The difference vector records separation, the cross product of path differentials records orientation, and cubic distance normalization weights contributions before double integration.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(Lk\) is the Gauss linking number of the two closed curves; \(C_1\) and \(C_2\) are the first and second closed curves or flux-tube paths; and \(\mathbf{r}_1\), \(\mathbf{r}_2\) are their respective position vectors.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry anchors topological counting with a precise invariant later pages may apply to linked flux or helicity models.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: The integer topological count of oriented winding between two closed curves.
The two paths over which the double contour integral is evaluated.
Preservation of linking under deformation that does not allow the curves to cross.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The double contour integral records oriented linking for the paired closed curves and supplies the invariant used by later topology applications.
